samples.xml_description:
<module id="traffic_light">
    <description>Implement a traffic light with red, yellow, green indicators and a pedestrian button. In normal traffic light operation, the east-west (EW) light should have a 60-clock cycle period, with 5 clock cycles of yellow and 10 clock cycles of red. The north-south (NS) light should have a 60-clock cycle period, with 5 clock cycles of yellow and 10 clock cycles of red. The cycle starts over when the pedestrian button is pressed. The cycle is only interrupted if the remaining green time is less than 10 clocks, in which case the cycle is delayed to at least 10 clocks. The east-west light is controlled by the pedestrian button and the north-south light is controlled by the east-west light.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="rst_n">Reset signal (active low).</input>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal.</input>
        <input id="pass_request">Pedestrian button input.</input>
        <output id="red">Red output signal.</output>
        <output id="yellow">Yellow output signal.</output>
        <output id="green">Green output signal.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <enum id="idle">Idle state.</enum>
        <enum id="s1_red">State 1 - red.</enum>
        <enum id="s2_yellow">State 2 - yellow.</enum>
        <enum id="s3_green">State 3 - green.</enum>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description>
        <sequential_logic id="traffic_light">
            <assign>
                <part id="case">
                    <cond>
                        <equal>
                            <var id="state"/> 
                            <enum id="idle"/>
                        </equal>
                        <true>
                            <assign>
                                <output id="red"/> 
                                <value>0</value>
                            </assign>
                            <assign>
                                <output id="yellow"/> 
                                <value>0</value>
                            </assign>
                            <assign>
                                <output id="green"/> 
                                <value>0</value>
                            </assign>
                            <assign>
                                <output id="state"/> 
                                <enum id="s1_red"/>
                            </assign>
                        </true>
                    </cond>
                    <cond>
                        <equal>
                            <var id="state"/> 
                            <enum id="s1_red"/>
                        </equal>
                        <true>
                            <assign>
                                <output id="red"/> 
                                <value>1</value>
                            </assign>
                            <assign>
                                <output id="yellow"/> 
                                <value>0</value>
                            </assign>
                            <assign>
                                <output id="green"/> 
                                <value>0</value>
                            </assign>
                            <assign>
                                <output id="state"/> 
                                <cond>
                                    <equal>
                                        <var id="cnt"/> 
                                        <value>[7:0] 3</value>
                                    </equal>
                                    <true>
                                        <enum id="s2_yellow"/>
                                    </true>
                                    <false>
                                        <enum id="s1_red"/>
                                    </false>
                                </cond>
                            </assign>
                        </true>
                    </cond>
                    <cond>
                        <equal>
                            <var id="state"/> 
                            <enum id="s2_yellow"/>
                        </equal>
                        <true>
                            <assign>
                                <output id="red"/> 
                                <value>0</value>
                            </assign>
                            <assign>
                                <output id="yellow"/> 
                                <value>1</value>
                            </assign>
                            <assign>
                                <output id="green"/> 
                                <value>0</value>
                            </assign>
                            <assign>
                                <output id="state"/> 
                                <cond>
                                    <equal>
                                        <var id="cnt"/> 
                                        <value>[7:0] 3</value>
                                    </equal>
                                    <true>
                                        <enum id="s1_red"/>
                                    </true>
                                    <false>
                                        <enum id="s2_yellow"/>
                                    </false>
                                </cond>
                            </assign>
                        </true>
                    </cond>
                    <cond>
                        <equal>
                            <var id="state"/> 
                            <enum id="s3_green"/>
                        </equal>
                        <true>
                            <assign>
                                <output id="red"/> 
                                <value>0</value>
                            </assign>
                            <assign>
                                <output id="yellow"/> 
                                <value>0</value>
                            </assign>
                            <assign>
                                <output id="green"/> 
                                <value>1</value>
                            </assign>
                            <assign>
                                <output id="state"/> 
                                <cond>
                                    <equal>
                                        <var id="cnt"/> 
                                        <value>[7:0] 3</value>
                                    </equal>
                                    <true>
                                        <enum id="s2_yellow"/>
                                    </true>
                                    <false>
                                        <enum id="s3_green"/>
                                    </false>
                                </cond>
                            </assign>
                        </true>
                    </cond>
                </part>
            </assign>
        </sequential_logic>
        <sequential_logic id="counter">
            <assign>
                <part id="case">
                    <cond>
                        <and>
                            <not>
                                <input id="rst_n"/>
                            </not>
                            <equal>
                                <var id="cnt"/> 
                                <value>[7:0] 10</value>
                            </equal>
                        </and>
                        <true/>
                        <false>
                            <assign>
                                <var id="cnt"/> 
                                <sub>
                                    <var id="cnt"/>
                                    <value>[7:0] 1</value>
                                </sub>
                            </assign>
                        </false>
                    </cond>
                </part>
            </assign>
        </sequential_logic>
        <sequential_logic id="output">
            <assign>
                <output id="red"/> 
                <var id="p_red"/>
            </assign>
            <assign>
                <output id="yellow"/> 
                <var id="p_yellow"/>
            </assign>
            <assign>
                <output id="green"/> 
                <var id="p_green"/>
            </assign>
        </sequential_logic>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
The first always block is responsible for the state transition logic. It uses a case statement to handle different states. Here's a summary of each state:
idle: Initial state where all signals are set to 0. Transition to s1_red state occurs immediately.
s1_red: Sets the red signal to 1 and waits for a count of 3 before transitioning to s3_green state. Otherwise, it remains in s1_red state.
s2_yellow: Sets the yellow signal to 1 and waits for a count of 3 before transitioning to s1_red state. Otherwise, it remains in s2_yellow state.
s3_green: Sets the green signal to 1 and waits for a count of 3 before transitioning to s2_yellow state. Otherwise, it remains in s3_green state.
The second always block handles the counting logic of the internal counter (cnt). The counter is decremented by 1 on every positive edge of the clock or negative edge of the reset signal. The counter values are adjusted based on various conditions:
If (!rst_n), the counter is set to 10.
If the pass_request signal is active and the green signal is active, the counter is set to 10.
If the green signal is inactive and the previous green signal (p_green) was active, the counter is set to 60.
If the yellow signal is inactive and the previous yellow signal (p_yellow) was active, the counter is set to 5.
If the red signal is inactive and the previous red signal (p_red) was active, the counter is set to 10.
Otherwise, the counter is decremented normally.
The assign statement assigns the value of the internal counter (cnt) to the output clock.
The final always block handles the output signals. It assigns the previous values (p_red, p_yellow, p_green) to the output signals (red, yellow, green) on the positive edge of the clock or negative edge of the reset signal.
</implementation>
</module>
